messages.author: dana.whitfield
messages.text: Hey — thanks for hopping on this. We need better visibility into shipment delays. Right now nobody can tell me which lanes or carriers are actually behind until a customer complains.

messages.author: dana.whitfield
messages.text: We've got shipments.csv from the TMS export and carriers.csv with each carrier's stated on-time rate. Whatever's useful, build it.

messages.author: dana.whitfield
messages.text: Also attaching delays_manual_tracking.csv — that's the sheet my team keeps by hand when we catch a delay before the system does. Fair warning, it's not the tidiest spreadsheet.

messages.author: you
messages.text: Got it. Starting with a look at the data — will follow up with what I find.

messages.author: priya.anand
messages.text: Dana looped me in. Before we talk dashboards — I want to understand *why* we're late so often. A pretty chart doesn't tell the board anything if we can't explain root cause.

messages.author: dana.whitfield
messages.text: Priya, I hear you, but my team just needs something we can check every morning. Root cause is a bigger lift than what we scoped.

messages.author: priya.anand
messages.text: We can figure out scope. I'd rather have one right answer than a dashboard nobody trusts.

messages.author: priya.anand
messages.text: Heads up — board meeting got pulled forward to this Thursday. Whatever you have, I need it a day earlier than we planned.

messages.author: dana.whitfield
messages.text: (to you) Sorry — we haven't sorted out whether this is a dashboard or a root-cause writeup. Use your judgment on what's most useful and we'll adjust.
